response.justification: The candidate is a clearly capable senior engineer with genuine geospatial interest (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G talk) and real low-latency systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and fibers), which maps loosely onto the pipeline performance aspects of this role. However, the core requirement of modern C++ or Rust is weakly met: his C++ experience is roughly a decade old and brief, his recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js product work, and he has no routing, navigation guidance, or map-data tiling experience. He could plausibly grow into the role given his systems background and demonstrated ability to switch stacks, but he is not a direct fit for the stated requirements.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G speaker)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work in DLang (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of req/s)
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, Linux/Docker/Kubernetes comfort
concerns: Modern C++/Rust requirement unmet — C++ exposure is brief and ~10 years old
No routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience
Recent roles are web/full-stack product work, distant from data-pipeline systems engineering
